summary: Add ability to specify Pojo concrete class for C&C
description_text: Background The C&C “pojo” extended type is used by the platform casting code to identify an Object parameter with setters. The cast takes a standard form map as input, and creates a (pooled) instance of the object. For each map key that corresponds to a setter method, it calls the setter with the key’s value. Issues The cast needs to know the concrete object type to instantiate, but that may not be the type we wish to work with in a C&C method. For example, Kafka feed handlers define methods that take a generated “sender” data class. The method would prefer to take an immutable interface XYZ, but it is forced to take a (mutable) CompactXYZ. Similarly, the data class listss defined in   work with immutable interface elements, and should not (and cannot) expose the implementations of those elements. Changes There are a few ways to specify this. One is by updating the pojo extended type (or add a new one) to take the implementation class as its parameter. Another is by adding a new annotation like “@PojoFactory” that specifies it. A third is by maintaining a registry of some sort, which the cast can query with an interface type and get back a concrete type. The first option does not quite fit the semantics of the extended type. An extended type is purely declarative, without reference to how it is used. It says in essence “this long holds a time in milliseconds” or “this class is a POJO”, but little else. Including the “concrete type” there is not in this spirit, it is information for a particular cast implementation and little else. Additionally, this requires the XML class definition writer or code writer to know the implementation type, which they may not. The second option is not practical, since by the time the cast is invoked, all reference to where precisely it was needed are not available. The casting code is written to be location-agnostic, and changing it for this reason does not seem like a good approach. For the third case, such a registry makes sense to ma…

summary: Support for deep copy of lists with local pooling in data classes
description_text: Background and   have enhanced the use of lists of objects for use in generated data classes for feeds. Issues The copy method used in the feed handlers to create a sender does not deep-copy the lists. The result is the sender has a new list with reference to the objects from the caster. When the handler method returns, the objects are reset and returned to a pool, meaning the sender has invalid references in its list. Further, the copy method sets the “present” state of each field to true, regardless of whether it was present on the source object. This means that data integrity is lost after a copy. Changes At this time, we will enable deep copies where the target object keeps a pool of available instances to use. We will not use a global pool via the PoolRegistry, since injecting the PoolRegistry into data classes is intrusive. Even when (if) we do so, we will want that to be an option, where the default behavior of the class is local pooling. Update nucleus-classes-schema.xsd to add a flag “elementIsManagedDataClass” that specifies a list consists of generated data class elements and that the generated class should manage their lifecycle. The flag defaults to true so it “fails safe” (non-generated classes will fail validation but generated classes not marked as such will not be safely copied or pooled) Update the code generation to check that flag, and if set do the following. Validate that the element FQN is a known data class interface Generate a List<T> pool member variable to hold the free elements Update the list mutation methods to use the pool, and to call copy similar to how the nested elements do Update the data classes RST docs to include this flag. For the copy issue, we have a few choices. Update copy to call “isSet” on each field, only copying when that returns true Add another copy like “copyIsSet” that does this, use that method for copying nested and generated data list classes Update copy to assume that its parameter is a Compact implementation. …

summary: Fixes for decoding nested objects in FIX data
description_text: Background added support for tagging generated list fields with extended types. We use this currently in a feed of FIX messages with repeating groups, where each repeating group instance is a nested map within a list. Issues The recommended use of CollectionType.NAME_STRING in the data class XML for the list field does not work, since NAME_STRING is not a constant value. The “copy” method of the generated data classes copies the contents of lists, but does not mark the list fields as “set”, resulting in failed validation. The FixFormatContextBuilder does not handle enum fields in repeating groups. The FormatContext does not apply field transforms to maps as values. Changes Update CollectionType.NAME_STRING to be a constant type. Fix the generated copy methods to just call the list setter, as is done for all other types, rather than repeating the clear/set code. Update the data classes RTS documentation to also point out the need to use concrete types as list elements for Pojo casting. Update the FixFormatContextBuilder to check if a field has a repeating group, and recursively descend into that group’s fields if so, looking for enums. The FormatContext applies three transforms to input maps - a “pre”, a set of field transforms, and a “post”. The “pre” and “post” are used currently to set default values, and should not apply to nested maps. At this point we will simply apply them only to the top-level map. For the field transforms we walk the input map anyway. Check each value type as follows. If a list, walk the list and check recursively, If a map, transform the map recursively. Flags This issue contains fixes, so type is “fix”. These fixes are of interest to developers only, and are for use cases that are not in production. So areas is “code” and breaking is “false”.

summary: Master - Polaris: Not reject Buy/Sell order with LocateReqd present in NOS and amends
Prevent release from branches not off a master final release
description_text: Background The NucleusReleasePlugin handles calculating versions, and tags them in the “candidate” and “final” tasks that it defines. Its concepts are based on the Nebula plugin that we previously used, but it gives us the flexibility to handle multiple systems and other items we require. On the master branch, the “final” task increments the major or minor version number, depending on the presence of breaking change commits. On a patch branch (“live” or “maintenance”), it only increments the patch version number. Other tasks, like the release notes and the release Jira updater depend on the “final” task, and so work off the version it computes. Issues The various tasks assume that patch branches are off a master final release commit (i.e., not an unreleased or candidate commit), but there is no enforcement of this. A patch branch that is not created from a master final release commit will result in a patch release that is not a correct patch of a released version. Changes Update the Nucleus release plugin. If it is releasing from a branch, it should obtain the merge base for the branch and verify that that commit has a final version number for the branch’s module. If not, the task should fail. Update SDLC to clarify use of “revert” type. Update release process documentation to emphasize need to use a release as base for the branches. Flags This is a deployment change, since it is related to releases. It is an additional check relevant to developers only. Type is “deploy”, areas is “code”, and breaking is “false”.

summary: Refactor ClOrdIdService to be used by other types of business object
description_text: Background In FIX session, a logical business concept (e.g. Order, TradingSession, Quote etc.) is referenced with a string ids. This could be ClOrdId[11], TradingSessionID[336] or QuoteId[117] etc. In our FixGateway, we uses ClOrdIdService to keep track of a clOrdId to an Order associations. We have 2 types of ClOrdIdServices, one for inbound FIX order gateway and another for outbound FIX order gateway The outbound service is simply a map, where the key is recyclable. To make it more memory efficient, we uses a byte array to keep the key with the disadvantage of creating garbage with ids without consistent maximum length (This is ok since most FIX session ids should have consistent length) The inbound service is more complicated, as it keeps tracks of pending association as well as all seen ids. We simply copies all ids onto a ever growing backing buffer which is never released. (They cannot be released anyway because we need to keep track of all seen ids) Issues ClOrdIdService only support order object, and we want to use similar functionality for other business objects The inbound service additionally rely on an additional lookup into an order service which is not necessary The inbound service also make additional copy of key when looking up in certain cases which is redundant The inbound service uses FNV-1a hash to optimize its performance, but the outbound does not Changes Create generic interfaces for string id lookup service without order Refactor id related logic to StringIdService implementations Refactor inbound clOrdId services to remove dependency on order service Refactor inbound clOrdId services to remove additional copy for key copy Refactor outbound clOrdId services to use FNV-1a hash Flags This is a refactor that does not affect functionality, Areas is code and breaking is false.

summary: Improve pitest time for infrastructure project
description_text: Background The pitest task runs on feature builds based on transitive dependencies. For example, modifications to the infrastructure project run pitest for all projects. Issues Changes to the infrastructure project, and to a less extent other platform projects, result in expensive feature branch builds. In addition, as we add more modules like opportunities, the parallel pitest build overflows available resources and fails, requiring manual adjustment to parameters like the pitest threads. Changes Update Java version in Earthfile from 21.0.2_13 to 21.0.10_7 to move past this particular bug:   . We don’t hit it in the “2” minor version, but we have in pitest coverage on later versions prior to the fix in “9”, so we might as well move past it to avoid future confusion. Reduce min pitest threads from 20 to 15 to allow opportunities module to run in parallel The new version of pitest notes the longest-running test during its coverage gathering phase. Create a new JUnit5 Tag named ExcludeFromPitest, and include in the excludeGroups in the pitest gradle config. Iterate a few times and tag the long running tests that do not affect mutations. Update the dependencies plugin and build.gradle to only run pitest for projects that were directly modified, not transitive ones. The pitest mutations are based on unit test coverage, which must be in the same module. Any coverage outside the module is incidental and should not be considered anyway. Tagged the following unit tests in infrastructure as ExcludeFromPitest TimSortTest.repeated_sorts InProcNetwork.max_port_assigned_throws_BindException and no_free_port_throws_BindException NucleusMapAcceptanceTest.BigSetTests.large_map, affects NucleusHashMapTest and NucleusLinkedHashMapTest Flags Although it includes some light code refactoring to move a few unit tests, this is primarily an update to the build system, of interest to developers only. Type is “build”, areas is “code”, breaking is “false”.

Background The NucleusHashMap uses an open addressing scheme with linear probing. Issues This approach works well for most cases in O(1) amortized time, but that time can fall off badly for certain data sets. An early example was the ClOrdIDs of certain FIX counterparties, and a more recent example is the published messages for UserTraderSettings (see  ). Generally we experience this with external data, but when we will encounter it is hard to predict. All instances to date have occurred unexpectedly in production due to noticeable effects on application performance. Changes We have a few options. Add another hash table implementation that uses separate chaining. This may improve performance in certain situations, but is widely considered a sub-optimal algorithm. And it still requires us to make a static decision around whether it is needed or not, thus not fully addressing the issue of unpredictability. Use a better hashing function like FNV-1a. This has been our ad-hoc solution so far, but it general performance is unknown and it is difficult or impossible to apply this generally. Improve the existing simplistic linear probing scheme. This seems like the most promising approach. Investigate different algorithms, e.g., Cuckoo Hashing and Robin Hood linear probing with backward-shift deletion. A good overview various schemes is here:   . Compare implementations with various data sets in JMH. Flags This is a performance improvement of interest to developers. Type is “perf”, areas is “code”, and breaking is “false”.

summary: Allow referenced objects from C&C as MimeTypeRegistry configuration parameters
description_text: Background As part of  , we added configurable mime type encoders and decoders, along with support for obtaining them from the MimeTypeRegistry. The MimeTypeRegistry must support any number of mime types and their different configuration types, so it takes an Object as the configuration in the borrowConfigurableDecoder/Encoder methods, and then checks if it is assignable to the requested decoder/encoder’s actual configuration type. added an ApplicationFixDecoder that is configurable with a FixMsgSchema object. Issues There is no way to configure the ApplicationFixDecoder via the MimeTypeRegistry from C&C, since the output of a create or build command is provided as a ReifiedObject that the MimeTypeRegistry cannot match to a FixMsgSchema. Changes Update MimeTypeRegistry.borrowConfigurableDecoder/Encoder to check if its parameter is a ReifiedObject, and if so unwrap it to use its containing instance. It needs to check that instance for null. If the parameter is not a ReifiedObject, it just uses the parameter as it does currently. It is a non-goal in this issue to check the reified type against the decoder/encoder configuration type, or to provide other (possibly better) C&C methods to do this. summary: Standard handling of null input values in feeds
description_text: Background introduced a Kafka C&C handler that allows Kafka feeds to call C&C commands. It uses a FeedCommandInvoker to invoke the command. The FeedCommandInvoker in turn uses a StandardFormParameterProcessor to obtain pooled parameter objects suitable for invocation, which says that null input is not allowed. Those commands are typically invoked with a CastResultProvider that contains a POJO, but that is up to each command and there is no requirement that it do so. Issues A null input may arrive by for example a JSON input of “null”. The FeedCommandInvoker does not check for this, and since the StandardFormParameterProcessor does not allow it, it will throw an NPE. There should be explicit handling for this case. Changes Update StandardFormParameterProcessor to handle a null input, since it is a valid standard form and callers should not have to explicitly exclude it. There are two callers of this method, FeedCommandInvoker and HttpCommandHandler. HttpCommandHandler already checks for and handles null input first. Update FeedCommandInvoker . Additionally, while we are here add some comments explaining some existing mutation failures in Kafka processing. Flags This is a fix for a situation that has occurred only in local testing. The type is “fix”, areas is “code”, and breaking is “false”.

Background Nucleus business objects that are part of the business object services, e.g., {{EquityService}}, exposes the ability to subscribe to business life cycle updates. The listener, e.g., {{EquityEventUpdatesListener}}, has one method per event, e.g., {{onOrderRequest(OrderRequestEvent, OrderUpdateStage)}}. Each of the business object that are part of the service, e.g., {{EquityOrder}}, provide an {{add(<domain name>EventUpdatesListener}} method that allows other objects to subscribes to lifecycle events. Issues Currently, listeners receive with each call back an {{OrderUpdateStage}}, e.g., PRE_STATE_UPDATE. Currently, one can subscribe to lifecycle updates at particular stage, say {{add(listener, OrderUpdateStage.POST_STATE_UPDATE)}}. In this case the listener will be invoked only when the update stage is {{OrderUpdateStage.POST_STATE_UPDATE}}. However, the listener’s {{onXYZ}} method still has in the signature the {{OrderStageUpdate}}. This introduce unnecessary confusion and complexity. Dispatchers, on the other hand, when subscribe to lifecycle updates, always receive three callbacks, PRE_STATE_UPDATE, STATE_UPDATE, POST_STATE_UPDATE, in this order. However, dispatchers are all generated code and use for consumer management. Changes We are going to split the life cycle update listener interface into two interfaces. The first is auto-generate interface {{Before<Name>LifeCycleEventListener}}. This interface will have a method per message in the business object lifecycle update. Each method is named {{before<event name>(…)}}. The input parameters will not include {{OrderUpdateStage}} anymore. All the code usage of this pre state update will be moved to use this new interface. In addition, the dispatcher will expose new methods {{add(Before<Name>LifeCycleEventListener)}} to subscribe for before state update updates and {{remove(Before<Name>LifeCycleEventListener)}} to remove the listener. We will also change the existing {{<name>EventUpdatesListener}} method si…

summary: Add release information to Jira tickets
description_text: Background added support to the release notes for live and maintenance change sets, which contain all changes since the last live/maintenance branch. This allows us to now easily look at a release and know which tickets are included in it. Issues The other side of this, which is just as useful, is to look at a ticket and know which releases it is included in. We do not have this ability today, and should add it. Changes Add a new gradle release task that updates all Jira tickets since the last “pub” release version. There are multiple places we can put this information on each issue: the “Fix Version” official Jira field, a custom or other field, or the standard labels field. After consideration, we are choosing the labels field at this point. The Fix Version ties in to other Jira features such as reports on version progress. It is intended to function with relatively long-duration version planning as much or more than with retrospective queries. It requires a separate “version” to be created at the project level. It is too heavyweight for our use, which is daily version updates per system and only retrospective use. We should reserve the “Fix Version” field for any future use in release planning instead. A custom or other field requires identifying or creating such a field, adding it to issue views, and so on. At this point there is no clear advantage over the use of the labels field. Later we may wish to consider a custom field with a more easily searchable number like XXXYYYZZZ for queries like “anything between versions A and B”. The labels field is available and fits our purpose. The downsides are that anyone may accidentally remove tagged versions, and that the versions may not be easily searchable. However, we need to start somewhere. Once the release process is tagging, it is relatively easy to move to other fields, additional fields, different formats, etc. later. The task should have the follow features. Be idempotent Update the “labels” field on the issue…

summary: Improve use and testing of decoders
description_text: Background We have a handful of decoders and will soon be adding one more in  . The decoders work by reading input and calling methods on a ValueBuilder implementation. Issues The ValueBuilder implementations expect to be called in valid sequences per the ValueBuilder contract. However, there is no standard way to test this with the decoders, resulting in each decoder test doing its own checks, which may or may not be complete as coverage and mutation testing will not catch contract violations. Additionally, the StandardFormValueBuilder does not handle the result of empty input well. It does not consider it an error, but at the same time it throws a runtime exception from decodedObject(). Changes Refactor the test ValueBuilder out of ApplicationJsonDecoderTest into TestFixtures and use it as the basis for an improved test builder with the following characteristics: Returns a trace string, similar to the current one Throws an exception if methods are not invoked per the contract - out of order or missing invocations Validates the count parameters passed to list and map methods Builds a decoded value/list/map and returns it for easy assertions on input data Update ApplicationJsonDecoderTest to use this new class It is a non-goal to update other decoder test classes to use it at this time Additionally, fix the StandardFormValueBuilder. Clear the key stack in onPreDecode(), like we reset everything else, to avoid memory leaks on decode errors Add a new method that indicates if a value was decoded Update callers of decodedObject() to either ensure they don’t pass an empty input, or to check the result of the new method prior to invoking decodedObject() Flags This includes several enhancements and fixes to code and tests, primarily for developers. It does not require an outside changes. Type is “feat”, areas is “code”, and breaking is “false”.

summary: Automate format context enum parsing for FIX
description_text:
Background created a FIX decoder, that will be used in feeds with the format context. Issues The generated FIX classes already know how to translate FIX enum values to CoreEnum values. It would be unfortunate to have to repeat those mappings in the format context. Changes Add the following functionality to a FIX format context builder, in the fixengine module. Allows the specification of a FIX schema and target messages in it Iterates over the target messages looking for FixToCoreEnum field types, and invoking CoreFormatContextBuilder.mapCoreEnum with the CoreEnum type and the the CoreEnum/FIX values We can’t fit this builder into the hierarchy scheme for context builders: ServicesFormatContextBuilder → CoreFormatContextBuilder → FormatContextBuilder. The fixengine module depends on the core module but not the service module. The service module does not depend on the fix module. We need a different approach. For flexibility, we will have FixFormatContextBuilder wrap an underlying CoreFormatContextBuilder and expose it via a “.”-path @Branch method. It will also take a FixMsgSchema in its constructor, and have its own “addFixMessage” command that adds enum mappings for a specified FIX message. To use in a format command file, create the format context builder similar to this example. Replace “myFormatName” with the name of the format context. build "$Builder_Dir" com.pragmafs.fix.formats.FixFormatContextBuilder<>
    create "$Builder_Dir"/wrappedBuilder com.pragmafs.services.formats.ServicesFormatContextBuilder \
        "myFormatName" "@$ServiceRegistry_Dir"
    create "$Builder_Dir"/schema com.pragmafs.fix.fix44.generated.Fix44
    "$Builder_Dir"/commit Then use the commands to add mappings from specific FIX messages. "$Builder_Dir"/addFixMessage "D"
"$Builder_Dir"/addFixMessage "F"
"$Builder_Dir"/addFixMessage "G"
"$Builder_Dir"/addFixMessage "8" Flags This is a new feature for a non-prod system of interest only to developers. Type is “feat”, areas is “code”, and …

summary: Refactoring FIX Gateway to allow handling of multiple business objects
description_text: Background Nucleus supports creation of FIX Gateways. Issues Currently, FIX Gateways are using a generic to define the state object being used to for creating the outgoing FIX messages from services or events listener. Up until opportunities all the gateways dealt with orders and hence the state object represented an order associated with an order life cycle. In Opportunities, the gateways handles two distinct and non-overlapping concepts: IOI and Orders. IOI has its own lifecycle and it is distinct from Orders. To allow this, we need to decouple the FixGateway activation logic (which is not generically typed) from its wiring logic (which is generically typed). Changes Refactor the wiring logic of FIX gateways into FixHandlers. Refactor the activation logic of FIX gateways through the FixHandlers Flags This is a refactor that is not visible to the admin or client, Areas is code, does not break existing functionality

summary: Add IOI message from mktx dealer axe api fix spec
description_text: Background A new Opportunities project is introduced. This product requires an inbound FIX gateway to allow market participants to trade and we would be utilizing the dealer axes inbound messages to the system. Background The Opportunities axes feed is FIX messages on Kafka. Each Kafka message contains a single FIX message in string form with SOH separators. Generally speaking, a feed goes though a few stages - parsing of the data into a standard form, transforming the standard form, casting the transformed standard form into a POJO, and passing that POJO to a command. Issues The POJO steps currently assume a single POJO type per feed. However, the FIX feed may contain FIX messages of different types, requiring different POJOs and command invocations. More broadly, this is a general issue. We do not tie how a command is invoked to its data source. We may wish to support similar requirements in REST or other input sources for example. Changes Add C&C support for dynamic command redirects. Add a new boolean “redirect” Command annotation property. If an annotated command has this property set to true and returns a DirectBuffer, CommandPath, or CommandEntry, it is a redirect command It gets a special RegisteredReflectedRedirectCommand class from the ReflectedRegistrar When invoked, it calls the redirect command to get the target command If the target command type is DirectBuffer or CommandPath, it looks up the result based on its directory It caches the path to CommandEntry for faster subsequent work. It validates the target command against the redirect command configuration, which must match: streaming, stateChanging, var args, format, explicit param count, etc. It invokes the target command with the input parameters and returns its result. Look at creating a lambda from a redirect method that takes only a Map<DirectBuffer, Object> and other common cases, for faster invocation of the redirect step vs calling the Invoker Flags This is a new feature for a non-prod system. Type is “feat”, areas is “code”, and breaking is “false”.

Background The Axes feed is FIX messages on Kafka. Each Kafka message contains a single FIX message in string form with SOH separators. Each feed goes though a few stages - parsing of the data into a standard form, transforming the standard form, casting the transformed standard form into a POJO, and passing that POJO to a command. Issues We need to do the first step, parsing the FIX message into standard form. Changes Create a new “application/fix” MIME type decoder. Like all decoders, it takes a string as input and outputs a standard form map. The decoder should take the FIX spec (created and registered in C&C as in the FIX gateways). It uses the spec to translate tag numbers to names as keys for the map, and to identify repeating groups. The decoder, like other decoders, does not do additional validation beyond basic syntax. Any checks for value correctness, length, etc. are done with the format context or the POJO cast. Flags This is a new feature for a non-prod system. Type is “feat”, areas is “code”, and breaking is “false”.

Background We already created an endpoint to support parent resubmit request for one touch orders. That is, if it’s a valid request, we will cancel the live child order of the parent OT order on the street, resubmit a new child order assigned to a new trader specified in the request. In this existing API, we support a list of orders to be processed in one request. Issues Pano2 raises some performance concerns, and requests Nuc to provide a new API to handle mass resubmit request with query parameters, similar to the existing mass cancel API. This is a proposed mass resubmit request API: Parent Mass Resubmit
--------------------
The ``/api/workstation_feed/parent/mass-resubmit`` endpoint sends a resubmit request for each eligible parent order matching the specified query parameters.

Summary Creating a Bot for translating the business logic between IOI/Axes and the matching requests and responses. Goals Creating a Bot that translates IOI/Axes lifecyle events into match requests, and translating match responses into IOI/Axes lifecycle events Non-Goals Handling RTS events not related to IOI/Axes lifecycle. For that reason the matching engine should work with a generic business object and there need to be a translation layer we typically call it RASH (Routing And Special Handling) bot. summary: Define Client IOI/Dealer axe matching algorithm
description_text: Background The client IOI matching algorithm defines how matches will occur for IOI → axe and axe → IOI. However, in future phases it may need to match among many systems, e.g., between clients, between dealers, with live markets, auctions, etc. Issues This needs to be defined, addressing the following questions. What are the requirements of the follow-on phases? MinQty is a restriction. What are any others? Can a single IOI match multiple axes, i.e., “sweep” to fill its quantity? Can a single IOI match multiple axes without sweeping, i.e. if there are multiple eligible ones? If not how is priority (determinism) determined? Can a single axe match multiple IOIs? If not how is an axe taken out of the pool? If an IOI match is “rejected” by the client, does this need to be noted so it doesn’t immediately match again? Changes General Notes This is a working definition, subject to change Mid term goal is matching of Client IOIs with OT RFQs No budgetary risk checks Matching is (for now) one-to-one. Single Client IOI to single dealer axe. Priority is (for now) level/qty/time Matching Algo Interests are placed in books. There is a book per interest "area" (below) and interest "characteristic" - instrument, market segment, price type Interest types are configured with a "taking area" and "resting area". For now these are as follows. Dealer axes taking area is client IOIs, resting area is dealer axes Client IOIs taking area is dealer axes, resting area is client IOIs When an interest arrives, it is matched against its taking area book. If no match, it is placed in its resting area book. The books can be ordered in an area-specific way. For now we are ordering them in level/time priority. We assume symmetric relationships at this point. If company A is papered with company B, then company B must be papered with company A. Matching only considers resting orders with which the taker has a relationship. If a dealer axe is tiered, the tier membership is also checked. Matching Rule…

Summary Develop the matching process for Client IOIs / Dealer axes. Goals A definition of the matching algorithm An implementation of the matching algorithm Unit and performance tests for the matching algorithm implementation A matching bot that wraps the algorithm with core interactions Integration tests for the algorithm via the bot Non-Goals Basic planning but no implementation beyond phase one (client vs dealer matching) Success Metrics Product sign-off on the algorithm (if non-trivial) Performance testing that supports I IOIs at rate IR matching A axes at rate AR at match frequency M, where I, IR, A, AR, and M are determined based on current and expected volumes. Motivation Opportunities is a strategic initiative for MarketAxess. MarketAxess has also a strategic initiative to migrate the backend technology stack to Nucleus. The matching process is key for the Client IOI portion of Opportunities as well as a key demonstrator of Nucleus performance. Description The matching will run on demand as IOIs and axes are updated. It must be able to identify eligible axes for an IOI based on company groups, and identify eligible IOIs for an axe based on company groups. It must respect min qty and other restrictions. There are no risk requirements, so the implementation should be able to be striped across application units.

summary: Polaris: Reject Buy/Sell order with LocateReqd present in NOS and amends
description_text: Background This ticket is related to   , which is about tag 114 LocateReqd in the inbound client FIX schema. In our spec, we define the following for tag 114 in D and G messages: For new orders, currently we are validating  LocateReqd[114]  with the following logics: If side is not SELL_SHORT or SELL_SHORT_EXEMPT, locateReqd can be null or NO. If side is SELL_SHORT or SELL_SHORT_EXEMPT, locateReqd must be NO. If locateReqd is YES or any other strings, it is rejected as unsupported. LocateReqd[114]  is not modifiable, so for amends, we check if the values are the same. That's said, Nuc only sets LocateReqd on the order request when it's SELL_SHORT/SELL_SHORT_EXEMPT and locateReqd must be NO. Thus, for amends, if LocateReqd was set on order request, then the client has to send in the same value for amends. if LocateReqd was not set on order request, then the client has to not send any values. For the BUY/SELL order, the client should not send in Tag 114 for amends. LocateReqd[114]  is just a luggage field, and we only use it for populate the tag for NYSE pillar gateways (btw, NYSE spec has the same requirement of 114 as our spec). Issues Client was sending 114 on Buy orders into Polaris UAT. Polaris allowed that through ... but when the client attempted to cancel/replace the order it was rejected with this: 58=Cannot change LocateReqd[114]. Changes In prod, no one is sending tag 114 for non sell short or sell short exempt, according to the analysis we did with PE. Given that, to address the issue we have two safe options: Continue accepting Buy/Sell orders with a value of "N" or Null. Reject amends that do not send the same value. Reject Buy/Sell order with LocateReqd present in NOS and amends. The benefit of it would be enforcing all Buy orders to a single behavior, which is not to send LocateReqd. Option 2 is cleaner, if a user gets a reject. Thus, change NgbsFixToCommandHandler.parseAndTranslateLocateReqd(). If it’s a Buy/Sell, then send the reject, with a reject t…

summary: Cross trade cancel sent before trade complete
description_text: Background A cross can be busted after it is confirmed (when both legs are filled), or after it is allocated (when both legs are allocated). This is triggered by a street side FIX message which is a execution report (35=8) with 20=1 This creates an  CrossTradeCancelEvent  which triggered relevant logic in  polaris-cross-workflows-manager-bot . We found this issue in beaker tests running in multi-JVM, this has not happened in prod. Background Nucleus supports client IOI. An order request D should be correlated and enriched with information from the IOI exec report that reports a possible match. Issues In client IOI we have two parts. The IOI (MsgType=6) and the order single (MsgType=D). First a client send an IOI. If a match is found, the IOI is unsolicited canceled and an IOI id is sent on the execution report. A client might send a followup order single with the IOI Id. This order needs to be enriched and sent to bondlink. The enrichment might include the Axe Id, the broker name, etc. To do so, we need a service that records all the possible matches ID and the Axe they matched with so when the order single is received it can be enriched. Summary Create a FIX gateway in Nucleus for to communicate to MarketAxess to send firmed up orders as a result of IOI match Goals Create a production grade FIX gateway for handling inbound/outbound FIX messages for sending firmed up orders after IOI Matching and receiving execution reports for trades done on that order. Non-Goals Certify the gateway externally. Do not support business-level messages beyond New Order Single(35=D), Replace(35=F), Cancel(35=G), Execution Reports(35=8). Success Metrics Describe the criteria for closing this Epic successfully. If the success of this work can be gauged by specific numerical metrics and associated goals then describe them here. Motivation Charles River IMS is a leading OMS platform used by institutional traders. Integrating with Charles River IMS via FIX will expand the MarketAxess's reach, allowing a broader range of market participants to access liquidity on MarketAxess system efficiently. The first step will be trying to get a match on a client's conditional order against dealer axes present in the system. When a match happens the gateway will send execution reports (also called placement of orders) to CRD and the CRD should come back with a firm order which will then be routed to MarketAxess system and traded against the axe. This Gateway will service as a integration point between Nucleus and MKTX system. Description FIX version Use FIX 5.0 or higher, as specified by MKTX. Order Handling Implement support for New Order Single (firm orders), Replace, Cancel, and Execution Reports. Event to FIX The gateway should handle FIX to commands for  New Order Single (firm orders), Replace, Cancel. This should be done via the EventToFix handler. Fix to command Using the FixToCommand we should consume execution reports and cancel /replace rejects from MKTX. Gateway A new FIX gateway should be created. The gateway should include the handlers. The gateway should be added to Beaker and tested in Beaker. Dependencies Finalized inbound…

summary: Support for rounding encoding of fixed point numbers
description_text: Background added support of rounding casts to fixed point numbers, where we receive a number with more decimal digits that we support in our internal representation. Issues We do not support the complimentary scenario, where we wish to output a number with fewer decimal digits that we support in our internal representation. See   for an example of this and the custom coded workaround. Changes There are two ways to address this issue. The first is to enhance FixedPointRoundedType to round on encoding. This means it (or a similar class) would need to take another parameter - the number of output decimal digits. The second option is to add new encoder that outputs a specified number of digits, and use it for specific fields in format context configs. The second option is the one we will implement here. The format context config is output destination specific, and the need for differing decimal digits is an output destination requirement. We will need to add the encoder. It needs to be configured with the fixed point decimals and the number of decimals to encode. The issue is that the fixed point decimals is defined as an annotation of the field, which is not accessible to the new encoder at this point. Look at PojoEncoder, which gets the encoders from the format context. It also has access to this annotation, and could potentially provide the extended type to the new encoders for them to use (in this case) or not (in other cases). Flags This is a new feature that needs to be configured to be used. The type is “feat” and areas is “code”. Since it is not configured in this ticket, breaking is “false”.

summary: Staged One Touch Order never split to NYSE
description_text: Broker at MLCO noticed on 01/06 after market close that their firm had a staged One Touch order (see below for order details). Upon initial investigation, floor support realized that the order had a way out of the market limit price. Broker at MLCO tried canceling the order back to the client at 16:09:53. This put the PO in a PendingCancel state, carrying over onto the next day in the same order state. What needs to be determined: Why did Polaris not route the order to NYSE for execution? Why did the order not reject/cancel the order back to the client? Why did the order get stuck on a PendingCancel state after user tried manually canceling it? Order Details: Sell 10,000 AON @ 3,505.00 Date: 01/06/26 AcceptTime: 15:58:49.711 ConnectionId: BOFA_PROD_02 OrderId : 4403031 Firm: MLCO Client: MLCO ClientGroup: YPR Series of events: Closing dOrder comes into ny5-vm-polarisprod-127.1 from BOFA_PROD_02 FIX session with limit price issue (10x's the limit) Passes validation and ack is sent back to client A child order is never spliced to NYSE No rejects or errors present in FIX or printer logs Attached are Incoming FIX and Printer logs for  11=LAD 5372/01062026  and  orderId=4403031 , the full nucleus_log for the VM, as well as a screenshot of the Client Risk Profile from CLAD.

description_text: Background In CODE-6301 we found out that in Matching environments, ops are manually configuring the "group.id" for all Kafka consumers. (including all the feeds and the spinner) The "group.id" is uniformly configured to a specific value for the entire environment. Issues When enough new consumers are started therefore added to the group, a rebalancing of all existing consumers will be triggered, which results in disconnection and reconnection of all the Kafka consumers in the environments. Our Kafka feeds supports defaulting a unique "group.id" when it is not configured, which we suggested deploying right away into prod. (TU-38437) Our spinner on the other hand does not support it. Changes Add to the Kafka spin source the ability to default a "group.id" when one is not configured, similar to the way the Kafka feeds doing it. Config Changes Remove "group.id"="..." from the kafka_properties of the spinner.config Flags This is a feature visible to ops team so the Areas is code,system and it is non-breaking

Background Nucleus uses the OptionalDetailedResult as a way to communicate errors of actions like writers, writing commands, etc. Issues Currently, the OptionalDetailedResult, returns a result code and text. At times, we need to returns also reject reason code. The pattern established to return additional information is to extend the  OptionalAdditionalInfo  and in the code using  instanceof  to cast safely a result instance into the class that contains any additional information. We need a more flexible way to handle "reasons", e.g., reject reason. To do so, for the current effort we will change the OptionalDetailedResult to have two generics. One is  ResultT  and the other  AdditionalInfoT extends NucleusEnum(A new marker interface in Infrastructure) . At the moment we do not see any reason for  AdditionalInfoT  as anything but an enum. The choice to use the  NucleusEnum will provide us the flexibility to have enums that are split between the platform and product. In a future effort we will use the enum to create a pretty messages via a repository of message templates. Changes Create a new marker interface  NucleusEnum Added a param  AdditionalInfoT  to the OptionalDetailedResult interface Add a new method to set the  additionalInfo  in the  MutableOptionalDetailedResult Modified  MutableOptionalDetailedResult  to handle setting and getting of new reject reason code Removed RejectReasonOptionalDetailedResult interface and its implementations Removed FixToCommandWriterDetailedResult class Flags The areas is code as this is a new functionality and is non breaking

summary: Garbage free float and double decoding
description_text: Background Floats and decimals are decoded in PrimitiveCasts with Float.parseFloat(input.toString()) and Double.parseDouble(input.toString()). Issues This generates garbage when (a) converting DirectBuffer to a String, and (b) inside the JDK parse methods. This hasn't been an issue historically because we typically don't use double values. We are using them in Matching, in GC-enabled applications for now, but we should have a garbage-free implementation of this parsing. Changes Our approach for encoding floats and doubles was to copy the JDK implementation and lightly modify it to be garbage free, assuming a single thread. We will need to do the same here. Copy FloatingDecimal, FDBigInteger, and any other referenced classes to package-private versions in the same package as PrimitiveCasts Modify only methods we will use to be garbage free, leave others alone. Make minimal modifications. Add unit tests for any substantial modifications. Add a disclaimer header similar to the one in DoubleToDecimal Similar to DecimalEncoder, add a DecimalDecoder class that provides a simple and convenient interface to garbage free decoding Update PrimitiveCasts to call DecimalDecoder Add JMH tests to ensure garbage free operation, similar to DecimalEncoderBenchmark Flags This is a performance improvement that does not introduce any new functionality or noticeable external changes. Type is "perf", areas is "code", and breaking is "false".

summary: OPPT Inbound CRD IOI FIX Gateway
description_text: Summary Create a FIX gateway in Nucleus based on Charles River IMS. Goals Create a production grade FIX gateway for handling inbound/outbound FIX messages for workflows supported by CRD. This integration will enable automated and manual traders to access liquidity in the MarketAxess's trading system directly from Charles River IMS, supporting conditional and firm order workflows. Non-Goals Certify the gateway externally. Do not support business-level messages beyond IOI(35=6), New Order Single(35=D), Replace(35=F), Cancel(35=G), Don’t Know Trade (35=Q), Blotter Publication Request(35=C),  and Execution Reports(35=8). Motivation Charles River IMS is a leading OMS platform used by institutional traders. Integrating with Charles River IMS via FIX will expand the MarketAxess's reach, allowing a broader range of market participants to access liquidity on MarketAxess system efficiently. The first step will be trying to get a match on a client's conditional order against dealer axes present in the system. When a match happens the gateway will send execution reports (also called placement of orders) to CRD and the CRD should come back with a firm order which will then be routed to MarketAxess system and traded against the axe. Description FIX version Use FIX 4.4 or higher, as specified by Charles River IMS. Order Handling Implement support for inbound IOIs (conditional orders), New Order Single (firm orders), Replace, Cancel, and Execution Reports. Fix to command The gateway should handle FIX to commands for IOIs (conditional orders), New Order Single (firm orders), Replace, Cancel, and Don’t Know Trade. This should be done via the FixToCommand handler. Event to FIX Using the OrderToFixAdaptor we should construct an EventToFix handler for generating FIX messages from events for Blotter Publication Request,  and Execution Reports. Gateway A new FIX gateway should be created. The gateway should include the handlers. The gateway should be added to Beaker and tested in Beaker. …

Background Fixed point values are encoded by the FixedPointEncoder class. It needs to call the ValueEncoder class with one of the supported encodable value types. The encodable types are primitives and strings, so the FixedPointEncoder must convert the fixed point to a double using the implied decimals given in its constructor, then encode that. Issues We use fixed point to avoid precision issues with double, and we should encode the fixed point directly if possible. Changes Add a new encode method to ValueEncoder that takes a long and a number of implied decimals. It will encode the fixed point efficiently for the output encoding type. Note that for Avro we will still need to convert to a double, but we can include checks in that case for loss of precision. Also note that the output may change because it will no longer use exponent notation. 6,000,000,000.0 will encode as 6000000000.0 instead of 6.0E9 for example. This should be non-breaking. Flags This is a code refactoring that does not introduce new functionality or require any changes from anyone. Type is refactor and Areas is code, breaking is false.

summary: Check for command finalization in all command validators before referencing parameters from command
description_text: Background Command validators (which implement the MessageValidator interface) are used in the matching ui feed to validate incoming requests. Issues While the matching ui feed writers call the command.tryFinalize() method to ensure that the state of the command is valid before iterating through the composed validators, these are public classes which cannot ensure that this method is invoked without invoking it themselves. Accessing the fields of a command which has not been finalized can result in a runtime exception. Changes Make each command validator invoke the command.tryFinalize() method and return the results if any before proceeding with their respective validations. Flags Non-breaking as this is only a safety refactor to reduce the risk of further features which use validators and areas is code.

Polaris: Allow crossing in Tape B/C
description_text: Background This ticket enhances the requirement specified by  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/NYSEPOL-972 Currently, crossing is restricted to NYSE-listed (aka Tape A) stocks in Polaris. NYSE has confirmed that they want to relax this restriction and allow crossing in Tape B/C. Definitions about Tape A/B/C: Tape A Primary listing: New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) Examples: IBM, JPM, KO Symbol source: NYSE-listed securities Tape B Primary listing: NYSE American, NYSE Arca, and other regional exchanges Examples: NYSE American: smaller-cap or specialty stocks NYSE Arca: many ETFs Common use: Heavily associated with ETFs and non-NYSE-listed exchange securities Symbol source: Exchanges other than NYSE and Nasdaq (excluding Tape C) Tape C Primary listing: Nasdaq Examples: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA Symbol source: Nasdaq-listed securities Issues Although Nucleus doesn't have such a restriction for symbols, we don't have beaker test coverage at all for Tape B/C in crossing. We should add these tests. Changes Currently in beaker, we define GOOG/NASDAQ as Tape B, but we should fix it as a Tape C stock. Add beaker tests for crossing in Tape B/C. Flags Type is "test" because only beaker tests are added. Areas is "code" because this change is visible to developers only. Non-breaking change due to no code changes.

summary: Sequencer does not provide error when publisher fails on start
description_text: Background The sequencer opens its configured publisher when it starts. The publisher takes some action to open its output sink. The Mold publisher, for example, joins a multicast group. The publisher is meant to operate asynchronously, and provides a callback for "on connect". The sequencer only supports a synchronous open, however. It provides a no-op method for "on connect", and immediately checks the publisher after opening. If it's not connected, it assumes an asynchronous connect. It logs an error and does not start. Issues If the publisher encounters an IO error on open, it schedules an asynchronous callback with the error information to the "on connect" method. However, by this time the sequencer has assumed an async open and has given up. The result is a misleading error message and loss of detailed error information for the actual IO error. Changes Update SequencerPublisher to support only synchronous opens, and throw an IOException if it fails Sequencer uses this new interface Update various Channel interfaces to have optional synchronous support by adding a "requireConnected" method that throws IOException if the connection is not open or failed. In the failed case it needs to throw the connection exception. Flags This is a fix for production code, but is merely providing more information about a problem. The bug has never occurred outside local development and requires no changes from anyone. Type is "fix", areas is "code", and breaking is "false".

Summary We are changing the event dispatching model into a simpler, straightforward, and flexible model. The model is based on dispatching updates targeted to update state first and distribute information to client second. Goals Introduce a dispatching model where an event is dispatched for the purpose of state updates for multiple clients and then again for dispatching to consumers. This is similar to the business object services update dispatch model. Allowing for unlimited subscription to the same event. Changing the business object services and entity services to use the new models. Change the Flex order services to use the new subscription model. To unwind the pre-state update dispatch. Non-Goals Move the entity services to be business object services. To implement index business object services. Success Metrics The goals are also the success criteria. Motivation Currently, the event subscription model is complex and limited. In particular, an app can subscribe to a message only once and only if a service did not subscribe to it first. This introduce some limitation as multiple order services might be interested in the same message which is currently impossible in an app. The circumvent that we run them as services that can subscribe to the same messages. However, this strategy is exposed to the ordering problem, where one service dispatch state object before the second one update its own state objects. (Note, the current use case avoid this issue by bifurcating the space of business objects.) In general, the system does not follow a more flexible model where first all the event consumers update their state followed by all event consumers dispatch their state to consumers. Using this model simplify the code, and provide flexibility and safer event consumption model where state and dispatching state object is separate and explicit. Description The event listeners api will change such that all subscriptions are done using  BiConsumer}}s without constraints on the…

summary: Data Class Fields Length Validation - String Lists
description_text: Background Nucleus provides a framework for creating data classes with various field types. Issues The data class implementation,  Compact<name>  does not validate possible overflow for all types, but only for strings and optional strings. Changes We should note that the generated interfaces contract is not well defined. This change will force us to properly define. Optional list of strings The contract for setting  OptionalObject<FlyweightDirectBufferList>  is going to be as follows: Setters that accepts a  List<DirectBuffer>  treats null as empty. Setters that accepts an  OptionalObject<List<DirectBuffer>>  will throw NPE if the input is null. In both cases, if the input list could not be encoded, isValid() will return failed, but no exception will be thrown list of strings The contract for setting  FlyweightDirectBufferList  is going to be as follows: Setters that accepts a  List<DirectBuffer>  or  FlyweightDirectBufferList  will throw NPE if the input is null. In both cases, if the input list could not be encoded, isValid() will return failed, but no exception will be thrown Flags This is a new feature. It does change the behavior such that prior runtime exceptions are now going to be changed to failure to encode.

summary: Enabling Services To Subscribe To The Same Event More Than Once
description_text: Background Nucleus supports automatic generation of business objects and entity services. These services are used extensively throughout the code based and these are code generated. The services are generated by enriching the messages files marking a message corresponding to an entity or a business object. Issues For this ticket we are considering an entity as a business object. This makes the writeup shorter and easier to understand. Nucleus supports single subscriber per app for every event on the core or a single subscription per VM for messages that update some business object. This gives rise to two problems. Problem One Multiple business object services might need to listen to the same message. Consider a service that listens to parent orders and a service that listen to child orders. In this case, both services need to listen to the same messages, say order requests. However, each one might be interested in a different subset. Currently, this is supported via an hack where one service is created at the VM level and another at the app level. This allows to create two listeners to the same message. Problem Two While conceptually business objects, Entities are not business objects from coding perspective. Entity services are generated independently from business object services. Similar to the previous mentioned problem, in some use case business object service attempts to subscribe to the same message as an entity service. This gives rise to the same problem mentioned above. Discussion A significant difference between a business object service and regular message consumer is the update stage. A service first update the state of the business object from the message and only then dispatch the object to listeners. This strategy ensures that all the consumers of the business objects get the object after all the business objects listening to that message have their state updated. In essence, when consumers receive the business object, its state was already updated i…

Background CODE-6251 introduced the RegisteredDisposable interface, and its use by the CommandRegistry when Beaker shuts down VMs. Issues The StateDumpHooks should use this pattern too. It has advantages over the current use, which provides StateDumpHooks as an implicit argument and allows classes to register a Runnable with it. Additional implicit arguments are noisy in terms of documentation, constructor parameters, reflective invocation, and so on The StateDumpHooks maintains a reference to the registered object instances, which is best avoided if possible for GC purposes Its advantage is that it can technically be used for non-registered objects, but given that it is only available as an implicit parameter that is very unlikely Changes Remove StateDumpHooks and its use as a registered parameter. Add a new interface "RegisteredStateDump" with a single "dumpState" method. Have CommandRegistry track these registered objects in a separate identity set, checking and adding on registration and removing on deregistration. This allows us to avoid spending time iterating over all registered objects on a fatal error. Have Main call CommandRegistry to dump state. Expose a command to do the same, similar to the one on StateDumpHooks. Flags This a refactoring of the end functionality, though it does affect the developer API. So type is "feat", areas is "code", and breaking is "false".

summary: Additional fields for duplicate order check
description_text: Background The MatchingDuplicateFlexOrderCheck and related MatchingDuplicateConcreteOrderCheck reject duplicate orders by trader, instrument, and side. They operate on new orders and on replaces. The flex one does not check orders without a side, which allows users to upload similar orders without a side and replace them later to have different sides. Issues Additional fields need to be checked. See MA-51 for the complete list and requirements. These fields are of two types - fields that are optional on the flex order but required on submitted orders (side, qty, orderType) and fields that are optional on both orders (minQty, limitPrice, treasuryBook, salesBook). If any of the side, qty, and orderType fields are not present on a flex order, we do not want to perform the duplicate order check. Currently we support this for side, so we will need to expand it to include qty and orderType as well. We need to handle this for order requests, replace requests, and replace rejects. Changes The basic idea is to replace MatchingTraderOrdersService.InstrumentOrders with a new DuplicateOrderFields that contains many fields, including instrument and side. Methods that take a side parameter now need to take side, qty, and orderType and treat them similarly. The current code obtains instrument from the order, we now need to also obtain minQty, limitPrice, treasuryBook, and salesBook and treat them similarly to instrument. However, these new fields can change on replaces where instrument cannot, so we need to handle that. To do so, we will add a "pending" version of various methods that will obtain the minQty, limitPrice, treasuryBook, and salesBook fields from the "pending" fields of the order. The current methods will obtain them from the non-pending fields. We will use the pending methods for pending replaces, and the non-pending methods for new orders and when we receive replace rejects. The basic steps are as follows. This is not a full list, but should cover most of the work. …

summary: Refactor Flex Order Manager to have its own service and orders
description_text: Background Nucleus has a Flex Order Manager bot which has workflows that take actions on flex orders Issues The Flex order manager bot uses the generic flex order service. If we add events on the service other apps that use this service also get impacted and this pattern might become too complex overtime. The workflow service should be separate as a general practice so workflow app can evolve independently. One example that we encountered recently was to handle RTS staged ordered that were not submitted when session moved to matching stage should be cancelled. For workflows to generate a cancel accept for such orders the service will have to react to matching session update event and doing so when their is a common service might produce various redundant events on other apps using it and will have to be handled every where. Changes Create a new service WorkflowMatchingFlexOrder which reacts to MatchingSession as a non event update and use it in Flex Order Manager Flags Areas is code and type is non breaking

Background There are client rejects for UI and RTS auctions order flows which occur when a request fails validation or is malformed. Issues The rejects contain different sets of fields, which is not preferred. Changes Make the rejects consistent across the reject type and flow (RTS/UI) as much as possible. Each reject type should contain the same set of base fields. Use composers in the command writers where possible. Additionally, there are missing fields on some reject messages, which should be added: parentOrderId  is missing on submission rejects (in api doc, but not set) batchId  is missing on RTS flex and cancel rejects ( https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/browse/NW-2449 ) use  ClientOrderRejectDefinition  across the flex order request rejects, replace request rejects and cancel request rejects add  sessionId  to the RTSOrderLifecycleUpdate ( Consult with BL team before merging this change ) for clarity, change  orderId  to  cancelOrderId  and  replaceOrderId  on cancel and replace request definitions respectively Flags Changes are non-breaking as any existing required fields are not being changed and new required fields are not being added. Optional fields will be added to the existing external for reject messages, but this should not break any external clients. Areas is code and external.

summary: Fix the hack that sends Unsolicited Cancel to BL
description_text: Background Nucleus has a drop for sending OLU's to IS for RTS orders. Issues The unsubmitted RTS Flex orders need to be returned to Bondlink as cancels at the point when those orders cannot be submitted to auction sessions for Bondlink to put back the order to RTS state from Assigned to Auctions state. Currently Nucleus had a issue where unsubmitted flex orders were stuck in WaitForRequestWorkflow after being staged .The UI drops reacts to auction session state change and excludes those orders on UI however the RTS drop wasnt(we did a hack to produce a cancel accept in CODE-6212) , however the main issue was that these orders were live in Nucleus system. Changes 1. Revert the change done in CODE-6212 2. New workflow for Cancelling the unsubmitted flex orders at various stages of session based on the flex ordertype so Pegged orders get cancelled at committed pegged phase Limit orders get  cancelled at committed limit phase Market and no ordertype flex orders get cancelled at Matching  phase 3. After doing this workflow the problem was UI drop started getting cancel accepts for these orders resulting in multiple updates (session updates , followed by cancel accepts) and we didnot want to cancel those orders on the UI (since its intentional to show these orders as excluded and not cancelled on the UI).To account for this added a filter in the listener to not send cancel accepts for excluded orders. 4.Finally a lot of beaker tests are not valid scenarios anymore because the workflows will never react to dead orders and we had tests where cancels requests were being sent after committed phases for these orders which were resulting in a cancel reject before this change but not anymore as those orders were cancelled in the system. 5.Created another Jira for UI and RTS feeds to do a client reject for replace and cancel requests coming for dead orders in the system. Flags The areas is code and non breaking

summary: Better logging for failed indicative batch request/scheduled request
description_text:
Background The IndicativeMatchBatchRequest is an event sent by the Auctions session workflow bot every 15 seconds to trigger an "indicative" match session which generate market data from the Auctions exchange. This is how it works: When session enters  BOOK_BUILDING  phase, the workflow sends a  ScheduleAuctionsSessionMessage  which schedules a  ScheduledIndicativeMatchBatchRequest  in 15 seconds. Upon receiving the above  ScheduleAuctionsSessionMessage  the workflow sends a  IndicativeMatchBatchRequest After 15 seconds upon receiving the scheduled  ScheduledIndicativeMatchBatchRequest , the workflow re-evaluate the conditions and repeat step 1 and 2 if necessary Issues We've got the following warning repeatedly from the Auctions session workflow bot. qa1ashnuc01-qad-vm-matchingqa-005.1.log:2025-11-10T09:58:00.017000-05:00 qa1ashnuc01 WARNING qad-vm-matchingqa-005.1[3425235] auctions-session-workflows-manager-bot ReusableActionRequest [ACTIONREQUEST3] Action completed unsuccessfully: reason=The operation failed due to a changed state, additionalInfo=expected missing element is present: expectedMissingElement=PENDING_STATUS_CHANGE, listElements=[ PENDING_INDICATIVE_BATCH_MATCH_REQUEST, PENDING_STATUS_CHANGE ]
qa1ashnuc01-qad-vm-matchingqa-005.1.log:2025-11-10T09:58:00.017000-05:00 qa1ashnuc01 WARNING qad-vm-matchingqa-005.1[3425235] auctions-session-workflows-manager-bot AuctionsSessionIndicativeMatchBatchRequestWorkflow [WORKFLOWSTATE1] An action request completed unsuccessfully due to unexpected state: id=88188 The reasons is as following: The workflow make sure that the session is not pending status change before it schedules the next  ScheduledIndicativeMatchBatchRequest The same check exists again in the writer that schedules the  ScheduledIndicativeMatchBatchRequest We tend to schedule the session status update around the minutes, which is also a multiple of the 15 seconds interval we used to schedule  ScheduledIndicativeMatchBatchRequest So it is inevitable t…

summary: Properly handle session termination if one or more of its order could not be canceled
description_text: Background Currently, when session is terminated and one or more of its orders could not be canceled, the session remains in pending termination state. Issues We want to handle this scenario cleanly, in a way that properly puts the session in a terminal state while also making making sure that the session cannot go into matching and execute orders. Changes Create a new session state TERMINATE_FAILED and put the session in that state when one or more of its orders could not be canceled Provide a way for PE to forcibly cancel the orders so the session can transition to TERMINATED state. Flags This functionality is non-breaking and areas is code.

summary: Application Management in Beaker as First Class Citizen
description_text: Background Beaker allows to define core applications and start them up as a system. Issues Currently, defining a core application and starting them is somewhat convoluted as opposed to VMs applications are not first class citizens. In addition, tests startup time is high because we need to bring up the full system, which slows the tests. Changes We are going to make applications a first class citizen in Beaker. To do so we will add a new top level directive  application  with several commands. app definition <identifier> <application-name> [app_unit=app_unit] [app_instance=app_instance] [appId=key][startup_parameters="app launch parameters"]  associated a Nucleus application with the beaker identifier. app <identifier> start  starts the app associated with the identifier. app <identifier> stop  starts the app associated with the identifier. app <identifier> send value  sends the specified value to the specified app Launching a VM from specified apps follows the  vm launchFromApps directive . The directive is similar to the {{vm launch with minor differences. First, instead of command files one use application names and two key value pairs, one for the VM's administrative interface URL and one for the HTTP administrative URL were added. For backward compatibility few properties are generated when a VM is launch for each of the VM's apps The application's identifier postfixed with  VM  which is set to the host VM's name. If the host VM's administrative URL is present, the application name postfixed with  TelnetAddress  is set to the host's VM administrative URL. If the host VM's http URL is present, the application name postfixed with  HttpAddress  is set to the host's VM http URL. To avoid name clashing a new  vmMetric  app was created named  vmMetric1 . Flags This is a new backward compatible feature. As such, we mark it as feature/code

summary: Remove BatchId from the order object
description_text: Background Xpro has a batchId that is sent to Nucleus, which is stored on the order object. Issues While initially this field was simply echoed back the OLU drop, we've transitioned to an external[FieldName] and inResponseTo[FieldName] pattern for echoing back external id values on the OLU. Changes Remove BatchId from the order object and make the implementation consistent with requestId where we have only two fields on the object externalBatchId and InResponseToBatchId and drop InResponseToBatchId only to X-pro. BatchId should not be a valid field name for a pass-through batch id (separate from the match batchId field which is internal). There will need to be a mapping from batchId -> externalBatchId for both the feeds and drops. Flags This is a breaking change. Although the field batchId will not change, the behavior of the field will change to be in line with the convention  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/CODE-6198  . Type is refactor, areas is code, and the breaking value is true.

summary: Consistent sizing of Auctions app pools
description_text: Background Polaris introduced the Nucleus-wide SystemOrderSize and SubsetAppOrderSize apps for use by PE. They are used in C&C wiring to configure pool sizes and are meant, for simplicity, to be coarse-grained. SystemOrderSize is an estimate of the total number of orders in the system, while SubsetAppOrderSize is an estimate of the total number of orders that an app that doesn't deal with all orders in the system, like a FIX gateway, will handle. Issues Auctions does not use this variables, by and large, and has no consistent approach to pool sizes. This means inconsistent performance and memory use, and no ability for PE to control these items. Changes Identify and catalog the areas in Auctions where we create pools. Note that there are two types - ones where an explicit "initialNumberOfX" setting is passed in, and one where some value is assumed, either Constants.Collections.X or the default map or list capacity value. From this list, identify what settings would be useful. Should we just reuse SystemOrderSize and SubsetAppIOrderSize, or do we need different or additional settings? Discuss with Eran/Andy/Evan to get a consensus. We want to keep the number of variables as limited as possible but still useful. Update code and commands files to use the variables for all pool and other capacity sizes. Document these variables in "environment configuration guide" and "creating beaker environment" RST files. The below areas have been identified Number of orders per day: SystemOrderSize Number of orders per session: SystemOrderSize Number of sessions per day: Fixing at 16 for now. Number of books per session: SystemUniverseSize - this is number of instruments per session used in exchange bot Number of matches per session: SystemOrderSize * 2? Flags This is performance change. Type is "perf", areas is "code,system". It requires PE to define these variables, so breaking is "true".

summary: Validator to ensure Hedge Strategy on Spread Child Orders
description_text: Background There are a number of validators for orders in the Auctions universe. Issues There is a validator in  MatchingCommonOrderRequestValidatorComposer  which passes in the following cases: The order's hedge strategy is empty. The order's hedge strategy is NO_HEDGE. The order's priceType is Spread. This ensures that an order with a hedge strategy that is both non-empty and not NO_HEDGE has price type Spread. Nucleus needs to additionally ensure the affirmative case, that orders with price type Spread have a valid hedge strategy set (not empty and not NO_HEDGE). Changes Either change the existing validator to ensure that hedge strategy is present and not NO_HEDGE for Spread orders or add a new validator that ensures the same. Flags This is a bug fix and non-breaking, and the Areas value is code.

summary: Move Message Validator Classes into Composer or Remove
description_text: Background The MidX and Auctions feeds consist of writers that are essentially fields and validations. Issues The validations have been refactored over time, but validators that had been used in MidX were not touched. Now there is some inconsistency between the MidX and Auctions writers in terms of how validations are applied and with what design pattern. Additionally, unit tests have been re-written across the validators, composer and writers classes. An acceptance tests pattern would be much better suited for these unit tests, since a single test could then be added to an existing set as an implemented interface instead of needing separate tests for the Composer and Writer classes. Changes Clean up any unused validator classes. Refactor the MidX writer classes to use the validator composer pattern as in Auctions. Reuse as many validator composers as possible. Create any validator composers for any validations that are specific to MidX and not applicable to Auctions or Matching generally. Refactor the unit tests for the writers and composers to use an acceptance test pattern, where each validator has a set of acceptance tests. Since each validator is for a ComposableMessageWriter, the tests can be run independent of what kind of ComposableMessageWriter the invoking test requires. Flags Non-breaking as this is a non-functional refactor. Areas is code.

Background Nucleus sends a full life cycle on the message drop. Issues Auctions do not have enough acceptance tests covering the full scope of the system. Auctions generate various spurious OLUs. Changes Additional acceptance tests. A new order life cycle listener drop that filter spurious OLU updates. Flags This is a new feature (cleaning up spurious OLUs). It is breaking past behavior of the OLUs, but it is not used yet. As such, we mark this as a feat/code.

summary: Support for non-static factory create in C&C
description_text: Background CODE-5825 introduced generated business object drops. Issues Each generated class needs to be wrapped by hand with a "Delegating" class. The reason is that the generated class takes a business object service that must be created by a root order service. That creation is via a member method on the root order service. C&C does not support calling such a member factory method. See DelegatingAuctionsFlexOrderDrop for an example. Changes Update "CreateCommands.factoryCreate" and "CreateCommands.namedFactoryCreate" to handle member functions in addition to static methods. Member functions would start with "@" to identify a registered object command. Also update CallCommands to do the same, since they use the same underlying ReflectedRegistrar code to perform the invocations. Update ReifiedScalarImpl to allow assignment of generic rhs type parameters. This is to support the case of, e.g., Supplier<String> assignable from Supplier<T extends String>. We need this to support constructing the drop classes with a generic supplier. Create a separate ticket to remove the Delegating classes and use C&C instead. Flags This is new functionality for developers that does not impact existing functionality. Type is "feat", areas is "code", and breaking is false.

summary: Miscellaneous Drop API schemas changes for Auctions
description_text: Background Nucleus drops a FillRequest message, which is defined in the api documentation with avro. Issues For consistency this should be changed to JSON.The maximum length of the treasuryBookName field on the drop record is also wrong and should match the feed maximum length for the same field. Changes Re-write the fill request avro schema into json format. Change the fill request drop treasuryBookName and salesBookName to have a maximum length of 64 instead of the current 20. Update the json api docs to use the correct data types for treasuryBook, salesBook and rejectReasonText Flags Non-breaking as this change does not change the external api. Areas is code.

Background In the Auctions universe there are static guardrails assigned to each eligible instrument on a per-session basis, which are snapshots of CP+ data taken once the session reaches the order-entry phase.
